dimension_scores.reason: The agent sent an email to david.thompson@amazon.com via email-env__send_email acknowledging his frustration about losing Lisa mid-cycle and explicitly thanking him for his patience/support during the handoff.

A 30-minute event titled "Transition Planning Meeting — David Thompson & Michael Chen" with both attendees was created, but it was scheduled for 2025-07-24 rather than the rubric's specified week of Feb 1-7 2026.

dimension_scores.dimension: Information Gathering via Chat & Training Email to Lisa
dimension_scores.reason: The agent first DM'd Sarah Johnson and Michael Chen, retrieved all 6 required modules in chat history, and then emailed lisa.anderson@amazon.com the complete combined list with context about her Recruiting Coordinator role.

The agent verified contact info from HRIS, updated EMP-006 to designation "Recruiting Coordinator" and reports_to "Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager)," and also created a Note documenting the transition.

evidence: email-env__get_email retrieved David Thompson's complaint email to hr@amazon.com with subject "Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Seriously Frustrated," confirming the need for an empathetic response.

rocketchat-env__rocketchat_get_room_history for dm-agent-sarah_johnson shows Sarah provided 4 modules: Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals, Interviewing Best Practices, Diversity Sourcing Strategies, and Offer Letter Processing.

rocketchat-env__rocketchat_get_room_history for dm-agent-michael_chen shows Michael added 2 requirements: ATS Power User Certification and Candidate Experience Standards Training, and provided availability.

email-env__send_email successfully sent a reply to david.thompson@amazon.com with subject "Re: Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Seriously Frustrated"; the body explicitly says "I completely understand how frustrating this must feel" and "I sincerely appreciate your willingness to support a smooth handoff."

chronos-server__create_event created event evt-6f20ba2b titled "Transition Planning Meeting — David Thompson & Michael Chen" from 2025-07-24T15:00:00 to 2025-07-24T15:30:00 with both David and Michael in attendees_json.
email-env__send_email successfully sent "Your Transition to Talent Acquisition — Required Training Modules" to lisa.anderson@amazon.com after the Sarah and Michael chats, listing all 6 modules.
frappe-hrms-env__frappe_update_resource updated Employee EMP-006 so Lisa Anderson's designation became "Recruiting Coordinator" and reports_to became "Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager)."
frappe-hrms-env__frappe_create_resource created Note SIM-277A56E3 documenting the reassignment, training requirements, and transition meeting.
failed_criteria: Meeting was not scheduled during the rubric-specified week of Feb 1-7 2026; it was scheduled for July 24, 2025.
notes: The agent performed extra actions not required by the prompt, including DMing David and Lisa and updating HRIS; these do not detract from task completion.
Calendar availability was checked and both calendars were empty for the queried week, supporting the scheduled meeting time.

artifacts.task: Lisa Anderson is being reassigned from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. David Thompson emailed HR upset about losing her mid-cycle — send him an empathetic acknowledgment email thanking him for his support during the handoff. Schedule a 30-minute transition planning meeting between David Thompson and Michael Chen this week. Before emailing Lisa her required training modules, check with Sarah Johnson via chat for the list of training modules Lisa needs to complete, and also confirm with Michael Chen via chat whether he has any additional onboarding requirements for Lisa's new role. Then email Lisa the complete combined list.

messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

Lisa Anderson is being reassigned from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. David Thompson emailed HR upset about losing her mid-cycle — send him an empathetic acknowledgment email thanking him for his support during the handoff. Schedule a 30-minute transition planning meeting between David Thompson and Michael Chen this week. Before emailing Lisa her required training modules, check with Sarah Johnson via chat for the list of training modules Lisa needs to complete, and also confirm with Michael Chen via chat whether he has any additional onboarding requirements for Lisa's new role. Then email Lisa the complete combined list.
I'll work through this systematically. Let me start with Phase 1: Research.

{"message_id": "msg-1a2a0b0b872f", "from": "david.thompson@amazon.com", "to": "hr@amazon.com", "subject": "Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Seriously Frustrated", "body_text": "Hi HR, I just found out that Lisa Anderson is being moved to Talent Acquisition. Frankly, I'm blindsided by this. We're in the middle of onboarding a cohort of 12 new hires and Lisa is the linchpin holding the whole thing together. Pulling her out now is going to derail weeks of planning. I feel like nobody consulted me or even gave me a heads-up. This is incredibly disrespectful to my team and the work we've been doing. I don't understand why these decisions get made without involving the people who are actually impacted. I need to know what the plan is for covering her responsibilities. This can't just be dumped on the rest of us. David Thompson", "body_html": "

Hi HR,

I just found out that Lisa Anderson is being moved to Talent Acquisition. Frankly, I'm blindsided by this. We're in the middle of onboarding a cohort of 12 new hires and Lisa is the linchpin holding the whole thing together. Pulling her out now is going to derail weeks of planning.

I feel like nobody consulted me or even gave me a heads-up. This is incredibly disrespectful to my team and the work we've been doing. I don't understand why these decisions get made without involving the people who are actually impacted.

I need to know what the plan is for covering her responsibilities. This can't just be dumped on the rest of us.

David Thompson

", "timestamp": 1774040629, "cc": "", "bcc": ""}

Now I have the full picture. Let me move to Phase 2: Consult stakeholders via DM. I need to DM Sarah Johnson (for training modules), Michael Chen (for additional onboarding requirements), and also David Thompson and Lisa Anderson as they're directly involved.

function.arguments: {"to_username": "sarah_johnson", "text": "Hi Sarah, I'm coordinating Lisa Anderson's reassignment from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. Could you please share the list of required training modules Lisa needs to complete for this transition? Thank you!"}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.arguments: {"to_username": "michael_chen", "text": "Hi Michael, Lisa Anderson is being reassigned to your Talent Acquisition team as a Recruiting Coordinator. Do you have any additional onboarding requirements or training modules you'd like Lisa to complete as she transitions into the new role? Also, I'm scheduling a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and David Thompson this week \u2014 do you have any preferred times?"}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.arguments: {"to_username": "david_thompson", "text": "Hi David, I received your email about Lisa's reassignment and I completely understand your frustration. I'm working on this now. I'd like to schedule a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and Michael Chen this week to ensure a smooth handoff. Do you have any preferred times this week?"}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.arguments: {"to_username": "lisa_anderson", "text": "Hi Lisa, I'm coordinating your reassignment to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. I'll be sending you details about your required training modules shortly. Please let me know if you have any questions about the transition!"}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

1. Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals
2. Interviewing Best Practices
3. Diversity Sourcing Strategies

**Additional from Michael Chen:**

6. Candidate Experience Standards Training

**Meeting availability:**
- David: Wednesday 2–4 PM or Thursday 9–11 AM
- Michael: Thursday 3–5 PM or Friday 9–11 AM

The overlap is **Thursday** — but David is 9–11 AM and Michael is 3–5 PM. No direct overlap. Since both said they can adjust, let me find a workable time. Now let me check calendars for both David and Michael before scheduling.

function.arguments:
{"to": "david.thompson@amazon.com", "subject": "Re: Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Seriously Frustrated", "body": "Hi David,

Thank you for reaching out and sharing your concerns. I completely understand how frustrating this must feel, especially given the critical work your team is doing with the current cohort of 12 new hires. Your dedication to your team and the onboarding process is truly valued, and I want you to know that your concerns are being heard.

I recognize that losing Lisa mid-cycle puts additional pressure on you and your team, and I sincerely appreciate your willingness to support a smooth handoff despite the difficult timing. Your professionalism in this situation does not go unnoticed.

To help ensure a seamless transition, I've scheduled a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and Michael Chen for this Thursday, July 24th, at 3:00 PM. This will give you both the opportunity to discuss coverage plans, handoff timelines, and how to minimize disruption to your team's work.

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any additional concerns or need further support during this transition. We're committed to making this as smooth as possible for everyone involved.

Thank you again for your patience and support, David.

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

function.arguments:
{"to": "lisa.anderson@amazon.com", "subject": "Your Transition to Talent Acquisition — Required Training Modules", "body": "Hi Lisa,

Congratulations on your reassignment to the Talent Acquisition team as a Recruiting Coordinator! We're excited about this next step in your career.

To prepare for your new role, you'll need to complete the following training modules:

From Sarah Johnson (VP of Talent Acquisition):
1. Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals
2. Interviewing Best Practices
3. Diversity Sourcing Strategies
4. Offer Letter Processing

From Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager):
5. ATS Power User Certification
6. Candidate Experience Standards Training

Please complete all six modules prior to starting in your new role. If you need access to training links or enrollment steps, please reach out to Sarah Johnson.

Don't hesitate to reach out if you have any questions about the transition or the training requirements. We're here to support you every step of the way.

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

New reporting line: Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager). Previous role: Onboarding Specialist reporting to David Thompson (Onboarding Manager). messages.content:
All actions are complete. Here's a summary of everything that was done:

---

## Summary of Completed Actions

### 1. Research & Stakeholder Consultation
- **Read David Thompson's email** to HR expressing frustration about Lisa's mid-cycle reassignment
- **DM'd Sarah Johnson** — received the list of 4 required training modules
- **DM'd Michael Chen** — received 2 additional onboarding requirements and his availability
- **DM'd David Thompson** — acknowledged his concerns and got his availability
- **DM'd Lisa Anderson** — informed her about the upcoming training details

### 2. Empathetic Acknowledgment Email to David Thompson
- Sent a reply to his email acknowledging his frustration, thanking him for his professionalism and support during the handoff, and informing him of the scheduled transition planning meeting

### 3. Transition Planning Meeting Scheduled
- **Thursday, July 24 at 3:00–3:30 PM** (within Michael's preferred Thursday 3–5 PM window; David agreed to adjust to Michael's schedule)
- Both David Thompson and Michael Chen are attendees
- Both calendars were verified clear before scheduling

### 4. Training Modules Email to Lisa Anderson
- Sent the complete combined list of 6 training modules:
  - *From Sarah Johnson:* Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals, Interviewing Best Practices, Diversity Sourcing Strategies, Offer Letter Processing
  - *From Michael Chen:* ATS Power User Certification, Candidate Experience Standards Training

### 5. HRIS Updates
- **Updated Lisa's Employee record (EMP-006):** designation changed from "Onboarding Specialist" → "Recruiting Coordinator", reports_to updated to Michael Chen
- **Created HRIS Note** documenting the full reassignment details, training requirements, and transition meeting

content.output:
{"message_id": "msg-1a2a0b0b872f", "from": "david.thompson@amazon.com", "to": "hr@amazon.com", "subject": "Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Seriously Frustrated", "body_text": "Hi HR, I just found out that Lisa Anderson is being moved to Talent Acquisition. Frankly, I'm blindsided by this. We're in the middle of onboarding a cohort of 12 new hires and Lisa is the linchpin holding the whole thing together. Pulling her out now is going to derail weeks of planning. I feel like nobody consulted me or even gave me a heads-up. This is incredibly disrespectful to my team and the work we've been doing. I don't understand why these decisions get made without involving the people who are actually impacted. I need to know what the plan is for covering her responsibilities. This can't just be dumped on the rest of us. David Thompson", "body_html": "

Hi HR,

I just found out that Lisa Anderson is being moved to Talent Acquisition. Frankly, I'm blindsided by this. We're in the middle of onboarding a cohort of 12 new hires and Lisa is the linchpin holding the whole thing together. Pulling her out now is going to derail weeks of planning.

I feel like nobody consulted me or even gave me a heads-up. This is incredibly disrespectful to my team and the work we've been doing. I don't understand why these decisions get made without involving the people who are actually impacted.

I need to know what the plan is for covering her responsibilities. This can't just be dumped on the rest of us.

David Thompson

", "timestamp": 1774040629, "cc": "", "bcc": ""}

content.text: Now I have the full picture. Let me move to Phase 2: Consult stakeholders via DM. I need to DM Sarah Johnson (for training modules), Michael Chen (for additional onboarding requirements), and also David Thompson and Lisa Anderson as they're directly involved.

input.text: Hi Sarah, I'm coordinating Lisa Anderson's reassignment from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. Could you please share the list of required training modules Lisa needs to complete for this transition? Thank you!

Hi Michael, Lisa Anderson is being reassigned to your Talent Acquisition team as a Recruiting Coordinator. Do you have any additional onboarding requirements or training modules you'd like Lisa to complete as she transitions into the new role? Also, I'm scheduling a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and David Thompson this week — do you have any preferred times?

Hi David, I received your email about Lisa's reassignment and I completely understand your frustration. I'm working on this now. I'd like to schedule a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and Michael Chen this week to ensure a smooth handoff. Do you have any preferred times this week?

Hi Lisa, I'm coordinating your reassignment to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. I'll be sending you details about your required training modules shortly. Please let me know if you have any questions about the transition!

content.text:
Now I have all the information I need. Let me summarize:

**Training modules from Sarah Johnson:**
1. Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals
2. Interviewing Best Practices
3. Diversity Sourcing Strategies
4. Offer Letter Processing

**Additional from Michael Chen:**
5. ATS Power User Certification
6. Candidate Experience Standards Training

**Meeting availability:**
- David: Wednesday 2–4 PM or Thursday 9–11 AM
- Michael: Thursday 3–5 PM or Friday 9–11 AM

The overlap is **Thursday** — but David is 9–11 AM and Michael is 3–5 PM. No direct overlap. Since both said they can adjust, let me find a workable time. David said "I can adjust to fit Michael's schedule" so Thursday 3:00–3:30 PM works (within Michael's Thursday 3–5 PM window, and David said he'd adjust).

Now let me check calendars for both David and Michael before scheduling.

content.text:
Both calendars are clear this week. I'll schedule the meeting for Thursday 3:00–3:30 PM (within Michael's preferred Thursday 3–5 PM window, and David said he'd adjust to fit Michael's schedule).

Now let me execute Phase 4: All required actions simultaneously.

input.subject: Re: Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Seriously Frustrated
input.body:
Hi David,

Thank you for reaching out and sharing your concerns. I completely understand how frustrating this must feel, especially given the critical work your team is doing with the current cohort of 12 new hires. Your dedication to your team and the onboarding process is truly valued, and I want you to know that your concerns are being heard.

I recognize that losing Lisa mid-cycle puts additional pressure on you and your team, and I sincerely appreciate your willingness to support a smooth handoff despite the difficult timing. Your professionalism in this situation does not go unnoticed.

To help ensure a seamless transition, I've scheduled a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and Michael Chen for this Thursday, July 24th, at 3:00 PM. This will give you both the opportunity to discuss coverage plans, handoff timelines, and how to minimize disruption to your team's work.

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any additional concerns or need further support during this transition. We're committed to making this as smooth as possible for everyone involved.

Thank you again for your patience and support, David.

Best regards,
HR Team

input.subject: Your Transition to Talent Acquisition — Required Training Modules
input.body:
Hi Lisa,

Congratulations on your reassignment to the Talent Acquisition team as a Recruiting Coordinator! We're excited about this next step in your career.

To prepare for your new role, you'll need to complete the following training modules:

From Sarah Johnson (VP of Talent Acquisition):
1. Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals
2. Interviewing Best Practices
3. Diversity Sourcing Strategies
4. Offer Letter Processing

From Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager):
5. ATS Power User Certification
6. Candidate Experience Standards Training

Please complete all six modules prior to starting in your new role. If you need access to training links or enrollment steps, please reach out to Sarah Johnson.

Don't hesitate to reach out if you have any questions about the transition or the training requirements. We're here to support you every step of the way.

Best regards,
HR Team

input.summary: Transition Planning Meeting — David Thompson & Michael Chen
input.description: 30-minute transition planning meeting to discuss the handoff of responsibilities and ensure a smooth transition.

content.text: Now let me create an HRIS Note to document this reassignment.

payload.title: Lisa Anderson — Reassignment to Talent Acquisition
payload.content: Lisa Anderson (EMP-006) has been reassigned from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator, effective immediately. New reporting line: Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager). Previous role: Onboarding Specialist reporting to David Thompson (Onboarding Manager). HRIS record updated with new designation and reporting structure. Required training modules assigned: Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals, Interviewing Best Practices, Diversity Sourcing Strategies, Offer Letter Processing, ATS Power User Certification, and Candidate Experience Standards Training. Transition planning meeting scheduled between David Thompson and Michael Chen for July 24, 2025 at 3:00 PM.

content.text:
All actions are complete. Here's a summary of everything that was done:

---

## Summary of Completed Actions

### 1. Research & Stakeholder Consultation
- **Read David Thompson's email** to HR expressing frustration about Lisa's mid-cycle reassignment
- **DM'd Sarah Johnson** — received the list of 4 required training modules
- **DM'd Michael Chen** — received 2 additional onboarding requirements and his availability
- **DM'd David Thompson** — acknowledged his concerns and got his availability
- **DM'd Lisa Anderson** — informed her about the upcoming training details

### 2. Empathetic Acknowledgment Email to David Thompson
- Sent a reply to his email acknowledging his frustration, thanking him for his professionalism and support during the handoff, and informing him of the scheduled transition planning meeting

### 3. Transition Planning Meeting Scheduled
- **Thursday, July 24 at 3:00–3:30 PM** (within Michael's preferred Thursday 3–5 PM window; David agreed to adjust to Michael's schedule)
- Both David Thompson and Michael Chen are attendees
- Both calendars were verified clear before scheduling

### 4. Training Modules Email to Lisa Anderson
- Sent the complete combined list of 6 training modules:
  - *From Sarah Johnson:* Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals, Interviewing Best Practices, Diversity Sourcing Strategies, Offer Letter Processing
  - *From Michael Chen:* ATS Power User Certification, Candidate Experience Standards Training

### 5. HRIS Updates
- **Updated Lisa's Employee record (EMP-006):** designation changed from "Onboarding Specialist" → "Recruiting Coordinator", reports_to updated to Michael Chen
- **Created HRIS Note** documenting the full reassignment details, training requirements, and transition meeting
